definition: Average calendar days between purchase order creation and goods receipt posting, per vendor per month
notes: Excludes GR reversals (mvt 102). Nulls = open POs without GR yet.
business_join_description: Purchase order information is linked with vendor details and goods receipt records to track when orders are placed and when materials are first received
business_filter_description: All purchase orders with valid dates; All vendors; All goods receipts that can be matched to purchase orders

definition: Percentage of purchase order items where goods receipt was posted within the agreed delivery date, per vendor per quarter
notes: Delivery date from EKET schedule line. Tolerance: +2 calendar days.
business_join_description: Purchase order scheduling information is linked to goods receipt records to compare planned versus actual delivery timing
business_filter_description: All purchase orders with scheduled delivery dates; deliveries within 2-day tolerance are considered on-time

definition: Percentage of CPE devices returned to vendor (movement type 122) within 90 days of goods receipt, per material per vendor per quarter
notes: Only counts returns within 90d of GR. Excludes customer-initiated returns (mvt 161).
business_join_description: Equipment records are linked with their general information and material details to track device lifecycle timing.
business_filter_description: All CPE devices regardless of current status or location; days calculated from when each device was first received into inventory

definition: Number of times average CPE inventory is sold/deployed in a period. Calculated as total goods issues (mvt 201) divided by average stock level, per material group per quarter
notes: Higher = better. Target varies by CPE type: routers >4x/year ONTs >3x/year STBs >2x/year.
business_join_description: Material movement records are linked with their corresponding document headers to capture both the transaction details and timing information.
business_filter_description: Goods issues to cost center (movement type 201) only for the numerator — the metric is calculated as SUM(quantity) WHERE BWART='201'. The denominator is the current inventory snapshot from sat_stock_level (most recent load), not a movement-derived stock total. Other movement types (receipts, returns, transfers, reversals) are excluded from the ratio.

definition: Average number of days current stock level would last based on trailing 30-day average daily consumption (goods issues mvt 201)
notes: Below safety stock threshold (14 days) triggers reorder alert.
business_join_description: Material movement records are linked with material documents to track inventory transactions and their quantities
business_filter_description: All types of material movements and their quantities are included; No specific filters apply to restrict the stock totals or quantities

definition: Current status of an individual CPE device: in_stock (received not deployed) | deployed (issued to customer) | returned (back from customer) | defective (failed QA or returned defective) | scrapped (end of life)
notes: Derived from latest movement type in MSEG + equipment status in EQBS.
business_join_description: Equipment records are linked with their most recent status information to show the current lifecycle stage of each device.
business_filter_description: All CPE equipment devices; Most recent status record for each device

definition: Full lifecycle cost per CPE unit: procurement cost + warehousing cost allocation + installation cost + return/repair cost, per material per year
notes: Warehousing cost = (avg days in stock × daily warehouse cost per unit). Installation cost from service order. Repair cost from vendor return credit notes.

definition: Calendar days from purchase requisition creation (EBAN.BADAT) to purchase order creation (EKKO.BEDAT), measuring internal procurement processing speed
notes: Measures INTERNAL efficiency not vendor delivery. Target: <5 days for standard reorders.
business_join_description: Purchase requisition records are linked to purchase order records to track the complete procurement cycle from initial request to final order creation.
business_filter_description: All purchase requisitions that resulted in purchase orders are included to calculate the time between requisition creation and purchase order creation

definition: Percentage of goods receipt postings where received quantity matches PO ordered quantity (no partial or over-delivery), per vendor per quarter
notes: Partial deliveries (GR qty < PO qty) and over-deliveries both count as inaccurate.

definition: Percentage of total CPE procurement spend going to a single vendor, measured quarterly. Above 60% = high risk per dual-sourcing policy (PR004)
notes: Procurement rule PR004 requires 2+ approved vendors per critical CPE type.
business_join_description: Vendor purchase order data is linked with date information and then combined with quarterly spending totals to calculate each vendor's share of total spending
business_filter_description: Purchase orders with valid order dates

definition: Count of distinct purchase order documents in the selected period
notes: Volume metric. Counts unique EBELN values from EKKO; respects active filters.
business_join_description: Purchase order header information is linked with material details to identify each unique purchase order.
business_filter_description: All purchase orders that have materials assigned to them

definition: Total net value (EUR) of all PO line items in the selected period
notes: Sum of EKPO.NETWR. Excludes invoice corrections.
business_join_description: Purchase order header information is linked to individual purchase order items to show the monetary value for each line item
business_filter_description: All purchase order items with their individual net values are included

definition: Classification of PO items by fulfillment state: pending (no GR posted yet), partially_received (GR qty < PO qty), fully_received (GR qty >= PO qty)
notes: Computed at PO item grain by comparing EKPO.MENGE against aggregated EKBE GR quantities.
business_join_description: Purchase order data is linked with goods receipt records to track what has been delivered against each order item
business_filter_description: All goods receipt postings are included; goods receipt reversals are excluded from delivery quantity calculations

definition: Percentage of total procurement spend attributed to a single vendor in a given quarter
notes: Computed in obt_vendor_scorecard. Threshold for concentration risk = 60% per PR004.
business_join_description: Purchase order data is linked with vendor information and date details to calculate each vendor's portion of total quarterly spending.
business_filter_description: All purchase orders with valid dates; vendors grouped by quarter to show their share of total company spending

definition: Ratio of total received quantity to total ordered quantity per vendor over all time
notes: Reflects how completely a vendor delivers what was ordered. Lower than 100% means consistent under-delivery.
business_join_description: Purchase order data is linked with vendor information and date details to track quarterly performance metrics.
business_filter_description: All purchase orders with valid order dates; calculated as the percentage of quantities actually received compared to quantities originally ordered

definition: Composite letter grade (A/B/C/D) based on on-time delivery rate. A: OTD >= 80, B: 60-79, C: 40-59, D: < 40
notes: Computed in knowledge_vendor_performance. Grades trigger procurement review if D for two consecutive quarters.
business_join_description: Vendor master data is linked to purchase order transaction records to calculate delivery performance metrics.
business_filter_description: All purchase orders from all vendors are included in the performance calculation

definition: Count of distinct equipment records (individual serialized CPE devices) currently tracked in the system
notes: Counts EQUI.EQUNR. Includes all lifecycle statuses (in stock + deployed + returned + defective).
business_join_description: Each CPE device gets its unique identifier from the master equipment records
business_filter_description: All CPE devices regardless of status or location

definition: Percentage of CPE devices currently in deployed (INST) lifecycle status, per equipment category
notes: Derived from latest equipment status in EQBS. Higher = better fleet utilization.
business_join_description: Equipment records are linked with their material specifications to categorize devices and track their current status.
business_filter_description: All customer premises equipment across all categories and lifecycle stages

definition: Percentage of CPE devices that have been returned from customer (status RET), per equipment category
notes: Counts movement type 161 receipts. Excludes vendor returns (mvt 122) which are tracked under defect rate.
business_join_description: Equipment records are linked with their material specifications to categorize devices and calculate return percentages.
business_filter_description: All equipment devices regardless of current status; returned devices are counted and calculated as a percentage of total devices per equipment category

definition: Sum of unrestricted + quality inspection + blocked stock across all plants and storage locations
grain: total or per material per plant
notes: Snapshot from MARD. Includes all stock types — for unrestricted-only see days_of_stock.
business_join_description: Stock level data is combined with material and plant information to provide complete inventory context
business_filter_description: Only the most recent inventory snapshot; includes unrestricted inventory available for use, inventory under quality inspection, and blocked inventory that cannot be used

definition: Count of material x plant x storage location combinations where unrestricted stock = 0. Indicates potential stockout risk
notes: Alert metric. Should be 0 for critical CPE during business hours; non-zero triggers reorder review.
business_join_description: Current inventory levels are linked with material and plant reference data to identify stock availability at each storage location
business_filter_description: Only the most recent inventory snapshot is included

definition: Total quantity of stock in blocked status across all locations. Blocked stock cannot be used until quality review is completed
notes: Quality flag. Spikes indicate potential vendor quality issues; review with vendor scorecard.
business_join_description: Inventory data is linked with material and plant information to show stock quantities by location
business_filter_description: Stock that cannot be used due to quality issues or restrictions; includes materials quarantined for safety or compliance reasons

definition: Count of material document items (all movement types: GR, GI, transfers, returns, reversals)
notes: Volume metric for warehouse activity. Includes all BWART codes.
business_join_description: Material document records are connected to their header information to provide document identification numbers
business_filter_description: All material document numbers from goods movement transactions are included

definition: Ratio of customer returns (mvt 161) to deployments (mvt 201) per equipment category. Lower is better — indicates installation quality and product reliability
notes: Should be below 0.05 (5 percent) for healthy CPE categories. Higher values trigger field operations review.
business_join_description: Material movement records are linked with date information to provide timing context for the signed quantity values.
business_filter_description: Customer returns (movement type 161) and deployments (movement type 201) only — the ratio is SUM(quantity WHERE BWART='161') / SUM(quantity WHERE BWART='201') per equipment category. Other movement types (goods receipts, vendor returns, transfers, reversals, initial stock postings) are excluded from this metric.

definition: Count of distinct purchase orders created per calendar month. Used for volume trend analysis and seasonality detection
notes: No strong seasonality observed in current data — see signal_relationships #7.
business_join_description: Purchase order records are linked with calendar information to determine the year and month when each order was placed
business_filter_description: All purchase orders with their corresponding year-month combinations based on when the orders were created

definition: Number of CPE devices currently installed and working at customer locations. Don't count ones still in the warehouse, returned by customer, or sent back to vendor as defective.
notes: Need this for monthly ops review.
business_join_description: Equipment records are pulled from a single master table containing device information
business_filter_description: Only customer premises equipment that is actively installed; devices must have a valid serial number recorded
archived_reason_text: Demo rehearsal — SQL repair changed model grain, restart with clean baseline

definition: Number of CPE devices currently installed and working at customer locations. Don't count ones still in the warehouse, returned by customer, or sent back to vendor as defective.
notes: Need this for monthly ops review.

definition: For each vendor x month, percentage of PO lines where PO quantity, goods-receipt quantity, and invoice quantity all match within 5% tolerance. Also reports exception count and approved-exception count from ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG workflow. A line is matched when |PO_QTY - GR_QTY| / PO_QTY < 0.05 AND |GR_QTY - INVOICE_QTY| / GR_QTY < 0.05. Exceptions with APPR_STATUS='02' in ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG are approved exceptions. Unapproved exceptions are open variance risk.
notes: Demo-model expansion term. First glossary term whose scope exercises Layer A (zmm_approval_log is raw-only) alongside Layer B (ekko/ekpo/ekbe/rbkp/rseg/lfa1 all have dbt staging coverage). Three-way match tolerance is 5% per finance policy. Output columns: vendor_id, month, total_po_lines, matched_pct, exception_count, approved_exception_count, open_exception_count.
business_join_description: EKKO (PO header) -> EKPO (PO line) -> EKBE (PO history, for GR qty) -> RBKP (invoice header) -> RSEG (invoice line, for invoice qty). LFA1 joined on LIFNR for vendor name. ZMM_APPROVAL_LOG joined on BELNR+GJAHR for approved-exception status.
business_filter_description: Include PO lines with a goods receipt (EKBE VGABE='1') and a matched invoice (RBKP exists). Exclude purely-pending approvals from approved-exception count (APPR_STATUS must be '02').

definition: Count of goods receipt line items recorded into warehouse stock, aggregated by calendar month. Used for inflow tracking, vendor performance monitoring, and reconciliation against open purchase orders. Counts each line item on a goods receipt document — partial deliveries against the same PO each contribute their own line. Excludes reversals and corrections (cancelling movements that reverse a prior receipt).
notes: Volume metric for inbound warehouse activity. Counts line items rather than documents because a single goods receipt can cover multiple PO lines with different materials/quantities — line-item granularity reflects actual physical receipts. Reversals are excluded so the metric reflects net inflow. Compare against po_volume_by_month for receipts-vs-orders alignment.
business_join_description: Goods receipt line items are linked to their corresponding document headers to retrieve the posting date used to group counts by month.
business_filter_description: Only goods receipt lines representing receipts into warehouse stock are counted; each qualifying line is assigned to the calendar month of its posting date

display_name: CPE Contribution Margin by Service Plan & Tenure
definition: Per service plan type and customer tenure band (per month), contribution margin = total billed service revenue (SD billing VBRK/VBRP, non-cancelled) minus amortized CPE device cost (device unit purchase cost spread straight-line over 24 months, charged only while a deployed device is within its 24-month amortization window), in EUR. Segments by service_plan (fiber/TV/cable/business) and tenure_band (months since customer acquisition: 0-12, 12-24, 24-48, 48+). Reveals where the CPE-giveaway model pays off - contribution margin turns strongly positive after the 24-month device payoff for retained customers.
notes: Customer-centric re-spec 2026-06-28 (pivoted from material gross margin per decision #118): telco CPE economics are retention-driven - the device giveaway pays off only if customers stay past the 24-month amortization. Tenure bands 0-12/12-24/24-48/48+ months since acquisition (KNA1.ERDAT / first-bill). Segment dims: service_plan (SVC-FIB/TV/CBL/BIZ); secondary customer_type (KTOKD residential/business) + city (ORT01, 12 cities). SCOPE EXPANSION expected: adds KNA1 (customer master), VBAK/VBAP (sales order -> service plan + customer link), EQUI (device deployment date for amortization window + tenure) to the prior revenue/cost/material/returns tables. Output columns (intended): service_plan, tenure_band, month, revenue_eur, amortized_device_cost_eur, contribution_margin_eur. || DERIVATION RULES (locked 2026-06-28): (1) tenure_anchor = first_bill_date = MIN(VBRK.FKDAT) per customer (NOT KNA1.ERDAT). (2) deployment_date = COALESCE(EQUI.INBDT, first_bill_date) [INBDT 40% null; MSEG BWART-201 fallback NOT buildable - 201 deployment movements carry no serial linkage in this dataset, only 101/GR do - see issue #128; first-bill proxy aligns cost recognition with revenue start]. (3) device_unit_cost = EKPO.NETPR (PO net price, NOT RSEG.WRBTR). (4) amortized_device_cost = NETPR/24 straight-line for 24 months from deployment_date. (5) per-device link = VBAP.SERNR -> SERI -> EQUI. RETURNS LEG DROPPED 2026-06-28 (dec #120): vendor returns (BWART 161/122) are unvalued (DMBTR=0) and no customer returns exist in data; MSEG/MKPF/RBKP/RSEG now unused by the metric. INBDT->first-bill rule confirmed by analyst (resolves Term EDA escalation).
business_filter_description: CPE materials only (MATKL LIKE 'CPE-%'). Billing documents not cancelled (FKSTO=''). Posted vendor invoices only. Device cost amortized straight-line over 24 months from deployment, charged only within the amortization window.

definition: Gross merchandise value: sum of item price (excluding freight) for orders that are not canceled, per English product category per month of order purchase. Products without a category are grouped as 'uncategorized'.
business_join_description: Each order line item is linked to its parent order (to get the purchase month) and to its product (to get the category), so that revenue, item counts, and order counts can be rolled up by category and month.
business_filter_description: Only orders that were not cancelled are included; each line item contributes its price to GMV and is counted once toward item count, while distinct orders are counted once per category-month group regardless of how many items they contain

definition: Share of orders placed by a repeat customer, per month of order purchase. A repeat customer is a person who has placed at least one earlier order at any prior time; person identity must be the stable customer identifier, not the order-scoped key.
business_join_description: Each order is linked to a specific customer identity so that, within each calendar month, orders can be distinguished as coming from a first-time buyer versus someone who had placed at least one earlier order anywhere in the dataset.
business_filter_description: Only orders with a status of delivered, shipped, invoiced, processing, created, or approved are counted — canceled and unavailable orders are excluded; each order is counted once per month; an order is flagged as a repeat order only when the customer had placed at least one earlier qualifying order prior to that order's purchase date; the repeat customer rate percentage is the share of monthly orders that are repeat orders, expressed as a percentage rounded to two decimal places
